name: Branch Brief Bar
description: Turn the current git branch into a crisp review-ready brief with local status, touched areas, changed files, recent commits, and the next PR action.
short_description: Local git branch brief with review base, changed files, commits, and next PR action.
default_prompt: Use $branch-brief-bar to inspect the current git branch locally, choose a review-ready compare base, summarize upstream status, touched areas, changed files, recent commits, and the next review action, or build a compact macOS menu-bar brief that keeps the handoff honest.
audience: AI-assisted coders handing work from terminal sessions into review
Maintainers shipping branch updates across shell, PR, and chat
People who keep getting asked what changed and what should happen next
detail_lines: Reads local git state and turns branch name, compare base, upstream distance, touched areas, status-aware changed-file previews, and recent commits into one compact review brief.

Defaults feature branches toward a main-like review base such as the remote default branch, main, master, or trunk when their upstream is only the same remote feature branch, so the handoff stays PR-oriented instead of push-oriented.
Stays honest about what local git can prove and does not invent GitHub metadata, CI state, or reviewer status.
